name: Parque Periurbano Fuente La Zarza — circular from Hinojosa del Duque
name_original: Parque Periurbano Fuente La Zarza — circular desde Hinojosa del Duque
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Hinojosa Del Duque traverse the Los Pedroches region, characterized by extensive dehesas featuring ancient holm oaks, recognized as Europe's largest and best-preserved holm oak dehesa. This area in the northern part of Córdoba province offers a tranquil backdrop for cycling. Natural features include rivers, peri-urban parks, and accessible hills, providing varied terrain for exploration. The landscape is generally rolling, with moderate elevation changes suitable for touring cyclists. Best touring cycling routes around Hinojosa Del Duque The most popular touring cycling route is La Granjuela from Peñarroya-, a 30.7 miles (49.4 km) trail that takes 3 hours 35 minutes to complete. This route offers a challenging ride through the expansive dehesa landscape. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Parque Periurbano Fuente La Zarza — circular from Hinojosa del Duque, a moderate 17.8 miles (28.7 km) path. This route explores accessible natural spaces close to the town. Local touring cyclists also love the Belalcázar – Castillo de Belalcázar loop from Belalcázar, a 30.3 miles (48.8 km) trail leading through varied terrain and past historic sites, often completed in about 4 hours 23 minutes. Touring cycling around Hinojosa Del Duque is defined by expansive dehesas, ancient holm oaks, and riverside paths. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to more difficult long-distance routes. The routes in Hinojosa Del Duque are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from more than 3 reviews. More than 130 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Hinojosa Del Duque's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Hinojosa Del Duque?
A: There are over 50 touring cycling routes available around Hinojosa Del Duque, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and preferences. You'll find everything from moderate loops to more challenging long-distance rides through the region's unique landscapes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Hinojosa Del Duque?
A: The routes primarily traverse the Los Pedroches region, characterized by expansive 'Dehesas' with ancient holm oaks, creating a tranquil and picturesque backdrop. You'll encounter generally rolling landscapes with moderate elevation changes, as well as paths alongside rivers and through peri-urban parks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1027131378/parque-periurbano-fuente-la-zarza-circular-from-hinojosa-del-duque" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque Periurbano Fuente La Zarza&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy about the routes in Hinojosa Del Duque?
A: The touring cycling routes in Hinojosa Del Duque are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars. Cyclists often praise the tranquil atmosphere of the dehesa landscapes, the well-preserved natural environment, and the variety of routes that cater to different abilities.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, there are several circular touring cycling routes. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1027131378/parque-periurbano-fuente-la-zarza-circular-from-hinojosa-del-duque" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque Periurbano Fuente La Zarza — circular from Hinojosa del Duque&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 28.7 km path exploring accessible natural spaces close to town. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38158991" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Granjuela – Ermita de San Isidro Labrador loop from La Granjuela&lt;/a&gt;, offering a moderate 33.9 km ride.

Q: What historical or cultural landmarks can I explore along touring cycling routes?
A: Hinojosa Del Duque is part of the historic Mozarabic Route to Santiago, offering a blend of cultural heritage and natural landscapes. Along the routes, you can discover landmarks such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5311931" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente El Pilar and Belalcázar Castle&lt;/a&gt;, the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5443321" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of San Juan Bautista of Hinojosa del Duque&lt;/a&gt;, and the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4667553" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza Mayor De la Villa de Dos Torres&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20128720" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Belalcázar – Castillo de Belalcázar loop from Belalcázar&lt;/a&gt; specifically leads past historic sites.

Q: Are there any notable natural features to see while cycling?
A: Absolutely. The region is famous for its extensive 'Dehesas' with ancient holm oaks, recognized as Europe's largest and best-preserved. You can also find routes alongside water bodies like the Pantano el Cascajoso and Embalse de la Colada. The Ruta del Río Guadamatilla is particularly noted for its spectacular scenery within the Los Pedroches region.

Q: What is the best season for touring cycling in Hinojosa Del Duque?
A: The best seasons for touring cycling in Hinojosa Del Duque are generally spring and autumn. During these times, the weather is milder, making for more comfortable rides through the dehesa landscapes. Summers can be quite hot, and winters can be cooler and wetter, though cycling is possible year-round with appropriate preparation.

Q: Are there options for less experienced touring cyclists?
A: Yes, Hinojosa Del Duque offers routes suitable for less experienced touring cyclists. While many routes are moderate, there are also easier options available. These routes often follow less challenging terrain, such as adaptable hiking trails like the Ruta del Río Zújar or Vereda del Camino Viejo, which have low difficulty.

Q: Can I find family-friendly touring cycling routes in Hinojosa Del Duque?
A: Many of the less challenging routes, particularly those with low difficulty and through peri-urban parks or along river paths, can be suitable for families. The generally rolling terrain and extensive network of trails provide opportunities for enjoyable rides for various ages and skill levels. Look for routes described as 'easy' or 'moderate' for family outings.

Q: Are there any routes that offer views of castles or reservoirs?
A: Yes, several routes provide views of historical castles and natural reservoirs. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38155443" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Belmez Castle – Sierra Boyera Reservoir loop from Peñarroya-Pueblonuevo&lt;/a&gt; offers scenic views of both a historic castle and a significant water body. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20128720" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Belalcázar – Castillo de Belalcázar loop from Belalcázar&lt;/a&gt; also passes by a notable castle.

Q: Is Hinojosa Del Duque known for any unique natural designations?
A: Yes, the Los Pedroches region, including Hinojosa Del Duque, was declared a 'Starlight Reserve' by UNESCO in 2016. This designation highlights the exceptional quality of its night sky due to low light pollution, offering a unique experience for those interested in astronomical observation, perhaps after a day of cycling.
tips: The name of this town is not much of a mystery, it seems that fennel abounded in the area and was originally called "Finojosa de los Pedroches" after the region in which it is located. The second part of the name is due to the fact that in 1553 the owner of the county, Francisco de Sotomayor, acquired this title when he married the III Duchess of Béjar, Elvira de Zúñiga.

It is a park close to Hinojosa del Duque and Valsequillo, with a series of small reservoirs, where the visitor can observe various species of birds from a perfectly conditioned public observatory. One of the main reasons for the protection of this site was the need to conserve the crane's wintering habitats.
